construct: Narrative gravity: a moment in the interviewee's telling that carries unusual analytic weight, marked by one or more observable discourse markers.

salience_scale.1: Marker present but incidental; does not shape the surrounding narrative.
salience_scale.2: Marker colors the passage but the narrative would read the same without it.
salience_scale.3: Marker meaningfully shapes how the passage should be interpreted.
salience_scale.4: Marker dominates the passage; the moment is a clear analytic anchor.
salience_scale.5: Marker defines the interview; a moment the analysis cannot ignore.

markers.name: Emotional display
markers.definition: The interviewee's language, delivery (as evident in wording/disfluency), or visible expression indicates a felt emotion tied to the content being narrated.
indicators: voice-adjacent text cues (e.g. transcribed laughter, sighs)

markers.name: Repetition
markers.definition: The interviewee returns to the same event, phrase, or claim more than once without being re-asked.

markers.name: Direct quoted speech
markers.definition: The interviewee re-enacts dialogue rather than reporting it ('and he said to me, ...').

markers.name: Tense/temporal shift
markers.definition: The telling breaks timeline: a jump into historic present, a flash-forward/backward, or collapsed time marking heightened involvement.
indicators: past narrative shifting into present tense

markers.name: Disfluency cluster
markers.definition: A localized spike in false starts, restarts, fillers, or trailing off, unusual against the speaker's own baseline fluency.

markers.name: Pause-then-rush
markers.definition: A marked silence followed by unusually rapid or dense speech, visible in the segment timing as a long inter-segment gap followed by high word density.
indicators: burst of tightly packed speech after a gap

rules: marker_types: non-empty list of marker ids from this codebook
emotion: required iff marker_types includes emotional_display; must be from the emotions list
quote: verbatim substring of the final transcript (the interviewee's words only)
t_start/t_end: seconds, within media duration, t_start <= t_end
visual_evidence: one of corroborates|contradicts|neutral, plus a short note — set only after frames are read
